mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and discount rates for future earnings, directly increasing equity valuations and generating positive forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are not significantly higher when FEDFUNDS is below its 12-month median.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.4 percentage points over three months signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer demand, triggering immediate negative equity returns within the next week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates synchronized strength in consumer and residential investment demand, driving sustained positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals yield negative 60-day returns in > 55% of cases, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample regression.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects capacity constraints and rising input costs that compress corporate profit margins, leading to negative forward equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening periods are followed by positive 20-day returns in > 60% of observations, or the signal loses predictive power when FEDFUNDS is rising.

mechanism_narrative: Higher total nonfarm payrolls reflect robust economic activity and rising aggregate income, which supports corporate revenue growth and generates positive short-term equity returns within the next week.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns show no significant correlation with PAYEMS levels.

mechanism_narrative: A year-over-year inflation rate exceeding 3.5% triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which increases discount rates and suppresses equity valuations over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite high inflation readings, or the threshold effect is absent in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between real GDP growth and personal consumption expenditures indicates structural weakness in household spending relative to overall output, signaling impending earnings revisions and negative forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The divergence metric yields positive 20-day returns in > 55% of cases, or the interaction term fails to improve model fit (AIC/BIC) over single-series baselines.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a post-recession recovery phase reflects expanding capacity utilization and improving corporate profitability, generating positive short-term equity returns within the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases during recovery phases are followed by negative 5-day returns in > 60% of observations, or the signal is statistically indistinguishable from zero.
